subject: History
overall_learning_objectives: Explain why European trading companies came to India
Analyse the importance of trading factories in early British expansion
Understand the causes and consequences of the Battle of Plassey
Evaluate the significance of the Battle of Buxar
Explain the concept of Diwani and its impact on Company power
Understand how the East India Company expanded its political authority in India

lesson_plan.title: European Traders in India
topics_covered: The East India Company and its objectives
student_activities: Identify European trading countries on a map
List important goods traded by the Company
Discuss why India was important for trade

lesson_plan.title: Trading Factories and British Influence
topics_covered: Meaning and purpose of trading factories
student_activities: Mark trading centers like Surat, Madras, Bombay and Calcutta on a map
Write the functions of trading factories
Discuss how trading settlements strengthened Company influence

lesson_plan.title: Battle of Plassey
topics_covered: Conflict between Siraj-ud-Daulah and the Company
Causes and results of the Battle of Plassey
student_activities: Create a cause–effect chart for the Battle of Plassey
Write the importance of the battle in 4–5 lines

lesson_plan.title: Battle of Buxar and Consolidation of Power
topics_covered: Background of the Battle of Buxar
student_activities: Compare the Battles of Plassey and Buxar
List the major results of the Battle of Buxar

lesson_plan.title: Diwani Rights and Company Administration
topics_covered: Impact on administration and revenue collection
student_activities: Explain Diwani rights in your own words
Discuss how revenue collection increased Company control

lesson_plan.title: Expansion of Company Rule and Chapter Revision
topics_covered: Transition from trade to political rule
student_activities: Prepare a flowchart showing the growth of Company power

evaluation_methods: Participation in map and timeline activities
teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyse how economic activities such as trade gradually led to political control in India by the East India Company.
